view.title: Named Port {hostname}
view.name: Pirate Name:
view.allGoodNoErrors: All is shipshape! No errors in contact information!
view.welcomeEmailConfirm: Are ye sure ye want to resend the welcome dispatch?
view.welcomeEmailResent: The welcome dispatch has been resent. Check yer crow's nest.

order.findDomain: Search the waters and check availability.
order.premiumAvailable: Arr! Yer named port {domain} be available and it is a premium port. Automatic claiming be disabled. Send a bottle to {email} if ye would like to purchase this port for {cost} per year.
order.premiumTaken: Avast! Yer named port {domain} has already been claimed! Ye already own it? Ye can transfer it, and it be a premium port. Automatic claiming be disabled. Send a bottle to {email} if ye would like to purchase this port for {cost} per year.
order.available: Arr! Yer named port {domain} be available! Ye can claim it for {cost}. Renewal cost will be {renewal}.
order.taken: Avast! Yer named port {domain} has already been claimed! Ye already own it? Ye can transfer it for {cost}. Renewal cost will be {renewal}.
order.subscriptionAutoRenew: The subscription will automatically weigh anchor again after every year at {cost} until ye cancel yer commission.
order.subscriptionDisclaimer: By checking this box, ye acknowledge that ye are purchasing a subscription product that automatically renews (As Per The Ship's Articles Outlined Above) and is billed to the Letter of Credit ye provide today. If ye wish to cancel yer auto-renewal, ye may access the captain's portal (Here), select the active vessel and click the Stand Down link or send a message in a bottle to: billing{'@'}interserver.net or use another method outlined in the Ship's Articles and Conditions. By checking the box and clicking Issue My Orders below, Ye also acknowledge ye have read, understand, and agree to our Ship's Articles and Conditions and Privacy Policy.
order.agreeCheckbox: I have read the ship's articles above and I agree.
order.searchingDomain: Patience, landlubber! Searching the waters for this named port.
order.loadingDomainFields: Patience, landlubber! Loading port registration fields.

nameservers.title: Port Navigation Servers
nameservers.suggestedApplied: Suggested port navigation servers have been applied. Click Update the Manifest to save.
nameservers.deleteConfirm: Ye be about to throw yer port navigation server overboard.

eppCode.title: Dispatch EPP Code
eppCode.confirmMessage: Are ye sure ye want to send the EPP Code to the registered message bottle address?

whois.title: Whois Privacy
whois.renewalNote: Whois Privacy gets renewed every 12 moon cycles from the date of activation. Whois Privacy addon renewal cost be {cost}.
whois.notAvailable: Whois Privacy be not available for this port extension.
whois.currentlyEnabled: Whois Privacy be currently set sail for {hostname}.
whois.disableAndCancel: Drop Anchor and Cancel Whois Privacy
whois.contactPrivacyDescription1: Contact Privacy hides the identity of a Port Owner when a crew member does a WHOIS lookup on that owner's named port.
whois.contactPrivacyDescription2: The benefit of having Contact Privacy be that the Port Owner's identity, including home port address, signal horn, and message bottle address, is shielded from scallywags, identity thieves and scammers.
whois.contactPrivacyDescription3: When Port Owners enable the Contact Privacy service, masked contact information appears in the public WHOIS database.
whois.contactPrivacyAvailable: Contact privacy be available for {cost} per named port per year.
whois.enableNow: To set sail with Contact Privacy for yer Named Port, Issue Orders Now.
whois.confirmOrderMessage: Whois Privacy doubloons: {cost} per year. Are ye sure ye want to place this order?
whois.enableMessage: Yer named port {hostname} has a settled whois privacy addon that be not yet active. Would ye like to set it sailing now?
whois.disableTitle: Drop Anchor and Cancel Whois Privacy
whois.disableMessage: Yer named port {hostname} Whois Privacy addon be currently sailing. Are ye sure ye want to drop anchor and cancel it?
whois.whoisDisabled: Whois Privacy has been anchored and cancelled.

whoisToggle.title: Whois Privacy Addon
whoisToggle.addonEnabled: Yer named port {domain} Whois Privacy addon be Sailing Full Speed
whoisToggle.addonDisabled: Yer named port {domain} Whois Privacy addon be Anchored
whoisToggle.confirmEnable: Are ye sure ye want to set Whois sailing?
whoisToggle.confirmDisable: Are ye sure ye want to drop anchor and cancel?

lock.title: Named Port Lock/Unlock
lock.activateLock: Do ye want to lock yer named port?
lock.inactivateLock: Do ye want to unlock yer named port?

dnssec.title: DNSSEC Information
dnssec.noRecords: No DNSSEC Records found in the ship's log.
dnssec.enterDigest: Enter Value of Digest (Must not be greater than {max} Characters)
dnssec.unableToParse: Unable to parse DS record format.
dnssec.confirmRemoveAll: This will throw all DNSSEC records for this named port overboard.
dnssec.savingRecords: Logging DNSSEC records in the ship's manifest...
dnssec.saveSuccess: DNSSEC records logged in the ship's manifest successfully!
dnssec.noRecordsWarning: Please fill in at least one DNSSEC record, matey.

renew.title: Renew Claim
renew.tip1: Named port should be renewed on or before the day of reckoning.
renew.tip2: If the named port has expired it may have a grace period from the expiry date to renew.
renew.tip3: Set sail with Whois Privacy to hide yer Contact Information when a crew member does a WHOIS lookup on that port owner's named port.
renew.alreadyRenewed: Arr, ye have already renewed yer named port!
renew.payExistingInvoice: To renew yer named port, kindly settle the existing Bill of Plunder.
renew.confirmRenewalMessage: Total doubloons: {cost}. Are ye sure ye want to place this renewal order?
renew.renewalOrderSuccess: Named port renewal order issued successfully!
renew.loadingRenewalInfo: Loading renewal information from the ship's log...

transfer.title: Transfer Status
transfer.notATransfer: This named port be not a transfer.
transfer.loadingTransferStatus: Loading transfer status from the ship's log...
